name: Salaries
description: Pay of Govt employees (FR 9(21)), honorarium, stipend to interns, emoluments/allowances of Heads of States, salary of Departmental canteen staff, leave encashment on LTC. Basic pay only — allowances moved to OH 07.

name: Wages
description: Wages of labourers and staff paid out of contingencies.

name: Rewards
description: Rewards under a scheme given to Govt employees in addition to pay and allowances. Includes bonus and cash awards for Hindi Pratiyogita etc.

name: Medical Treatment
description: Medical reimbursements/treatment of Govt employees and pensioners.

name: Allowances
description: DA, HRA, Transport Allowance, Foreign Allowance, Non-Practising Allowance, Deputation (Duty) Allowance, Personal Pay, Family Planning Allowance, Special Compensatory (Hill Areas/Remote Locality), Tribal Area, Hard Area, Headquarter, Overtime, CEA, Reimbursement of Tuition Fee, Ration Allowance, Cost of Ration in cash, Constituency, Uniform & Clothing, Entertainment, Project, Bad Climate, Washing, Special Duty, Night Duty, Risk, Sunderban, Cash Handling, Caretaking, Split Duty Allowance and any other allowance payable in addition to pay. [NEW w.e.f. 1.4.2023]

name: Leave Travel Concession
description: Air/rail/bus fare or any other mode of transport entitled under LTC Rules. [NEW w.e.f. 1.4.2023]

name: Training Expenses
description: Cost of training: fees paid, contingencies, materials for participating in training/workshops. Excludes domestic or foreign travel expenses. [NEW w.e.f. 1.4.2023]

name: Pensionary Charges
objectClass: Class II: Social Security of Employees
description: All pensionary benefits incl. pensions and gratuity in all forms to Govt employees, MPs, freedom fighters etc. Contributions to service funds and contributory provident funds. Leave encashment at retirement/death/termination. Govt contribution under NPS. Does NOT include social security expenditure such as old age pension. [Shifted from Class I]

name: Domestic Travel Expenses
description: Travel expenses on official tours and transfers within India. Includes TA/DA to non-official members on travel in India. Transfer TA payable to pensioners at retirement.

name: Foreign Travel Expenses
description: Expenses on official tours and transfers outside India. Includes TA/DA to non-official members going on official tour abroad.

name: Office Expenses
description: All recurring and non-recurring contingent expenses for maintenance of office establishment: stationery, postage, courier, telephone, internet, cable, electricity, water, service agreements, security, hiring of retired Govt servants on short-term contract, outsourced office attendants/DEO, house-keeping, liveries, hot/cold weather charges, pest control, refreshment, books and periodicals, hospitality incl. entertainment of foreign delegates, gifts, souvenirs, conferences/seminars/workshops/meetings (all related expenses incl. study material/kits, study tours). Purchase of office equipment, furniture and fixtures NOT exceeding ₹1 lakh or 3 years useful life (whichever reached first). Vehicles irrespective of usage go to capital OH 51.

name: Rent, Rates and Taxes for Land and Buildings
description: Rent for buildings (non-residential or residential) or structures, municipal rates and taxes, lease charges for rented land and buildings whose ownership is NOT transferable to Govt. [Lease charges where ownership IS transferable → capital OH 78 (Land) / 72 (Buildings and Structures)]

name: Royalty
description: Expenses on royalties on patents, designs, trademarks, print, publishing, music etc.

name: Printing and Publication
description: Printing of valuables, audit/accounts reports, forms, stationery, office codes, manuals, other documents, newspapers, magazines, e-books, e-magazines, digital printing, pen drive, CD etc. Excludes printing of publicity material (→ OH 26).

name: Rent for Others
description: Rent for equipment and other items: office equipment, transport, computer and ancillary equipment, communication, AC/heating/refrigeration, security, broadcasting/recording, construction, agricultural, horticultural, medical equipment, furniture and fixtures. Lease charges where ownership NOT transferable to Govt. [Lease charges where ownership IS transferable → respective capital OH]. [NEW w.e.f. 1.4.2023]

name: Digital Equipment
description: Hardware and software where cost of individual item does NOT exceed ₹1 lakh or 3 years useful life (whichever first). Consumables like toner and cartridge for printer classified under revenue regardless of cost. [NEW w.e.f. 1.4.2023]

name: Materials and Supplies
description: Various kinds of supplies, materials and stores: medical supplies, educational supplies, agricultural supplies, livestock supplies, cleaning materials, hospital drugs and medicines, veterinary drugs, chemicals and fertilizers, lab supplies, spare parts, clothing and tentage.

name: Arms and Ammunition
description: Revenue expenditure on arms and ammunitions for police and other para-establishments.

name: Cost of Ration
description: Procurement of ration provided to police and central armed police forces.

name: Fuels and Lubricants
description: Petrol, oil, lubricants and other fuels like CNG, diesel etc.

name: Advertising and Publicity
description: Commission to agents for sale and printing of publicity material; advertising through print media, TV, outdoor media, Internet or mobile network, other audio-visual media; fairs and exhibitions.

name: Minor Civil and Electric Works
description: Repairs and maintenance of minor civil and electrical works of office buildings, residential buildings, other buildings; expenditure on running operation and maintenance (ROM) of diesel genset etc. maintained by CPWD.

name: Professional Services
description: Engagement of professionals, consultants, artists, banks etc. for services to Govt: legal services, consultancy fees, audit fees, teaching/training fees, payments to artists, remunerations to question setters/invigilators/guest speakers, payments to other departments for services, payments to agencies for departmental examinations.

name: Repair and Maintenance
description: Repair and maintenance (incl. all maintenance contracts) of: machinery and equipment, office equipment, functional use equipment, digital equipment (office and functional use), furniture and fixtures (office and functional), vehicles (motor and non-motor incl. bicycle, rickshaw, cart, trolley, boat), infrastructural assets (preventive, operating maintenance of roads, bridges, rolling stocks, ships, aircrafts, helicopters, radars, hovercrafts, airports, other infrastructure, telecom towers), tools and plants, arms and ammunition. Excludes upgradation, midlife rehabilitation, retrofitting and reconditioning. [NEW w.e.f. 1.4.2023]

name: Bank and Agency Charges
description: Bank service charges, agency charges, MDR charges, direct benefit transfer charges to banks and any other charges for convenience fee performing monetary transactions. [NEW w.e.f. 1.4.2023]

name: Awards and Prizes
description: Expenses on awards and prizes given by Govt to eminent persons and organisations. [NEW w.e.f. 1.4.2023]

name: Grants-in-Aid — General
description: Grants-in-aid released for payments other than salaries and creation of capital assets. Includes expenditure on welfare activities.

name: Contribution
description: Contributions to international or national organisations related to membership. Does NOT include transfers to autonomous bodies or PSUs/PSBs for corpus funds.

name: Subsidies
description: Subsidies released under various schemes of the Government.

name: Scholarships
description: Scholarship amount released to various institutions, organisations, beneficiaries or individuals. Includes stipends to interns.

name: Grants for Creation of Capital Assets
description: Grants-in-aid released for payment for creation of capital assets. Includes Viability Gap Funding (expenditure on projects under VGF Scheme).

name: Grants-in-Aid — Salaries
description: Grants-in-aid released for payment of salaries.

name: Aid Material and Equipment
description: Value of aid material and equipment transferred to Ministries/Departments, other Govts or organisations. Includes grants given in kind to grantee bodies. [NEW w.e.f. 1.4.2023]

name: Secret Service Expenditure
description: Expenses on secret services.

name: Loss in Exchange
description: Loss due to difference in rate of exchange of foreign currency in Indian rupees. Loss at time of receipts/loans from foreign resources and repayment thereof.

name: Interest Payments
description: Payment of interest on capital and discount on loans.

name: Other Revenue Expenditure
description: Payment out of discretionary grants, other discounts, fees and fines, custom duty compensation, commitment charges, notional value of gifts, re-imbursement of newspapers at officers residence, purchase/re-imbursement of briefcase/ladies purse to Govt servants. Any expenditure not classifiable under any specified object head. Expenditure on schemes/sub-schemes/organisations not elsewhere classified. In place of old OH "Other Charges" (50) and "Lump Sum Provision" (42). [NEW w.e.f. 1.4.2023]

name: Motor Vehicles
objectClass: Class VI-A: Non-Financial Assets — Fixed and Intangible Assets
description: Procurement of motor vehicles on road: buses, cars, trucks, motorcycles, irrespective of usage (office or otherwise). All vehicle purchases are capital regardless of value.

name: Machinery and Equipment
objectClass: Class VI-A: Non-Financial Assets — Fixed and Intangible Assets
description: Procurement of machinery and equipment (other than motor vehicles and ICT equipment): electrical and electronic equipment, medical appliances, precision and optical instruments, watches and clocks, musical instruments, sports goods. Cost exceeds ₹1 lakh or 3 years useful life (whichever first).

name: Information, Computer, Telecommunications (ICT) Equipment
objectClass: Class VI-A: Non-Financial Assets — Fixed and Intangible Assets
description: Procurement of ICT equipment: computer hardware, telecommunication devices (computers/laptops, projectors), and computer software exceeding ₹1 lakh or 3 years useful life (whichever first). Electromagnetic spectrum used in transmission of sound, data and television. [NEW w.e.f. 1.4.2023]

name: Buildings and Structures
objectClass: Class VI-A: Non-Financial Assets — Fixed and Intangible Assets
description: Office buildings, residential buildings, other buildings and structures (hospitals, laboratories, auditorium, light houses, shelters), public monuments (statues, fountains at public places), and land improvement. Lease charges for buildings where ownership IS transferable to Govt. [NEW w.e.f. 1.4.2023]

name: Infrastructural Assets
objectClass: Class VI-A: Non-Financial Assets — Fixed and Intangible Assets
description: Infrastructural assets: roads, bridges, tunnels, irrigation projects, power projects, sports infrastructure, water and sewage projects, railway assets, ships, ports, satellites, satellite launch vehicles, airports, aircrafts, motor boats, railway locomotives and rolling stock, other infrastructural projects (cable lines, sewage systems, rain water harvesting, solar systems, telecom towers, transmission lines, electricity towers). [NEW w.e.f. 1.4.2023]

name: Furniture and Fixtures
objectClass: Class VI-A: Non-Financial Assets — Fixed and Intangible Assets
description: Expenditure on purchase of furniture and fixtures exceeding ₹1 lakh or 3 years useful life (whichever first) for office use and functional use. [NEW w.e.f. 1.4.2023]

name: Arms and Ammunition (Capital)
objectClass: Class VI-A: Non-Financial Assets — Fixed and Intangible Assets
description: Procurement of arms and ammunition of capital nature. [NEW w.e.f. 1.4.2023]

name: Upgradation/Procurement of Heritage Assets and not elsewhere classified
objectClass: Class VI-A: Non-Financial Assets — Fixed and Intangible Assets
description: Rehabilitation, overhaul, retrofitting of heritage asset recognised and recorded in the asset register at the nominal value of Re. 1/-. Upgradation "not elsewhere classified". Procurement of items of fine art and of cultural and archaeological importance. [NEW w.e.f. 1.4.2023]

name: Other Fixed Assets
objectClass: Class VI-A: Non-Financial Assets — Fixed and Intangible Assets
description: Procurement of other fixed assets: library books and publications, trees/crops/plants whose natural growth and regeneration is under direct control of institutional units, non-motor vehicles (bicycle, rickshaw, cart, trolleys, boat). [NEW w.e.f. 1.4.2023]

name: Land
objectClass: Class VI-A: Non-Financial Assets — Fixed and Intangible Assets
description: Land: ground, land for office and residential building, soil covering and any associated surface waters (reservoirs, lakes, rivers and other inland waters over which ownership rights can be exercised). Lease charges for land where ownership IS transferable to Govt. [NEW w.e.f. 1.4.2023]

name: Non-produced Assets other than Land
objectClass: Class VI-A: Non-Financial Assets — Fixed and Intangible Assets
description: Mineral and energy reserves on or below surface: oil, natural gas, coal, metallic ores (ferrous, non-ferrous, precious), non-metallic mineral reserves (stone quarries, clay and sand pits, chemical and fertilizer mineral deposits, salt, quartz, gypsum, natural gem stones, asphalts, bitumen, peat), water resources, plants yielding both once-only and repeat products where ownership rights are enforced but natural growth/regeneration is not under direct institutional control — virgin forests, commercially exploitable fisheries. [NEW w.e.f. 1.4.2023]

name: Intangible Assets
objectClass: Class VI-A: Non-Financial Assets — Fixed and Intangible Assets
description: Copyright, patents, goodwill, intellectual property etc. Acquiring ownership in copyrights, patents, goodwill, intellectual property (previously under Royalty). [NEW w.e.f. 1.4.2023]

name: Investment
description: Investments made by Govt: purchase of shares and equity, investment in securities, investment in fixed and term deposits, and other investments.

name: Loans and Advances
description: Loans and advances given by the Government.

name: Repayment of Borrowings
description: Repayment of borrowings by the Government.

name: Subscription
description: Subscriptions made by the Govt of capital nature. [NEW w.e.f. 1.4.2023]

name: Other Capital Expenditure
description: All other capital expenditure which cannot be classified under any of the above capital object heads.

name: Suspense
description: Amount kept under suspense heads for want of complete details for adjustment under final head of account. [Shifted from Class V]

name: Depreciation
description: Depreciation charged on assets by commercial departments.

name: Reserves
description: Provisions of reserves.

name: Inter Account Transfers
description: Used for transfer of amount from one head to another.

name: Writes Off of Losses
description: Write off of irrecoverable loans, trading losses.

name: Deduct Receipts
description: Amounts paid from the receipt heads by adjusting as reduction in receipts. [NEW w.e.f. 1.4.2023]

name: Deduct Recoveries
description: Operated to adjust the overpayments in reduction of expenditure.

name: Other Administrative Expenses
objectClass: Subsumed in OH 01 (Salaries), OH 49 (Other Revenue Expenditure), OH 09 (Training Expenses)

name: Clothing and Tentage
objectClass: Subsumed in OH 21 — Materials and Supplies

name: Other Contractual Services
objectClass: Subsumed in OH 49 — Other Revenue Expenditure

name: Lump Sum Provision
objectClass: Subsumed in OH 49 — Other Revenue Expenditure

name: Other Charges
objectClass: Subsumed in OH 49 — Other Revenue Expenditure

name: Major Works
objectClass: Subsumed in OH 71–79 (Capital assets) and OH 72/73 (Buildings, Infrastructure)
